skill.name: pptx-official
skill.description: Professional PowerPoint presentation creation, editing, and analysis using OOXML standards and HTML-to-PPTX conversion workflows.
skill.summary: Create, edit, and analyze PowerPoint presentations using OOXML manipulation and HTML conversion workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 1158 patterns but most are false positives. Markdown backticks flagged as shell commands, XSD schema enumerations flagged as C2 keywords, and standard library operations flagged as cryptographic risks. Actual risk is low: subprocess calls use hardcoded arguments for document conversion tools (soffice, pdftoppm). File operations are standard OOXML processing workflows. No user input injection vectors detected.

low_findings.title: External Process Execution for Document Conversion
low_findings.description: The thumbnail.py script uses subprocess.run to call soffice (LibreOffice) and pdftoppm for converting presentations to images. Commands use hardcoded arguments with no user input injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Subprocess calls are for legitimate document conversion tools with hardcoded arguments - standard pattern for document processing skills

low_findings.title: Zip Archive Extraction
low_findings.description: The unpack.py script extracts PPTX files using zipfile.ZipFile.extractall(). PPTX files are ZIP archives containing OOXML - this is the standard way to process them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Archive extraction is required for OOXML processing, defusedxml is used for safe XML parsing which mitigates zip slip risks

content.value_statement: Transform how you work with PowerPoint files. Create professional presentations from HTML or edit existing PPTX files at the XML level with complete control over slides, layouts, and content.
actual_capabilities: Create new PowerPoint presentations from HTML markup with precise positioning and styling
Edit existing PPTX files by unpacking, modifying XML, and repacking with validation
Extract text, comments, speaker notes, and design elements from presentations
Analyze presentation structure including slide layouts, masters, and themes
Generate thumbnail previews for visual validation of slide layouts
Convert between HTML slides and PowerPoint format using PptxGenJS library
limitations: Requires Node.js and Python runtime environments with specific dependencies installed
HTML conversion workflow requires Playwright browser automation for rendering
Thumbnail generation requires LibreOffice (soffice) and poppler utilities
Complex animations and transitions may not survive XML editing workflows

use_cases.title: Automated Report Generation
use_cases.description: Generate weekly status presentations from structured data by converting HTML templates to professionally formatted PPTX files with charts and tables.
use_cases.target_user: Business analysts and project managers who need to produce recurring presentations

use_cases.title: Presentation Remediation
use_cases.description: Fix corrupted presentations, extract content from damaged files, or batch-update branding across multiple slide decks using direct XML manipulation.
use_cases.target_user: IT support staff and document specialists managing enterprise presentation libraries

use_cases.title: Design-Consistent Slide Creation
use_cases.description: Create presentations that follow specific brand guidelines by defining color palettes, typography, and layout patterns in HTML before conversion.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and designers who maintain brand consistency across presentations

prompt_templates.title: Basic Slide Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new PowerPoint presentation with 5 slides about [TOPIC]. Use a clean professional design with a blue color scheme. Include a title slide, agenda, 3 content slides with bullet points, and a summary slide.

prompt_templates.title: HTML to PowerPoint Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the following HTML slide deck to PowerPoint format. Preserve all styling, colors, and layouts. Add a bar chart to slide 3 showing quarterly revenue data: Q1: 120K, Q2: 145K, Q3: 138K, Q4: 162K.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting web content or HTML prototypes to presentations

prompt_templates.title: Presentation Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this presentation file and provide: 1) Total slide count including hidden slides, 2) Color palette used in the theme, 3) Font families used throughout, 4) List of all comments and speaker notes, 5) Any slides with layout issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding existing presentation structure and content

prompt_templates.title: XML-Level Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Open this PPTX file and make the following edits: 1) Update the company logo on all master slides, 2) Replace all instances of the old product name with the new one, 3) Add transition effects between section slides, 4) Export a validation report showing any XML errors fixed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced editing requiring direct OOXML manipulation

output_examples.input: Create a title slide with 'Q4 Results' as the main heading and 'December 2024' as subtitle
output_examples.output: Generated Q4_Results.pptx with title slide featuring centered heading in 44pt font, subtitle in 24pt, with gradient background from navy to light blue

output_examples.input: Extract all comments from the presentation
output_examples.output: Found 12 comments across 5 slides: Slide 3 has 4 reviewer comments about budget figures, Slide 7 has 3 comments on timeline revisions, Slide 9 has 5 comments regarding design changes

output_examples.input: Create thumbnail grid for visual review
output_examples.output: Generated thumbnails_grid.png showing all 24 slides in 4x6 grid layout. Hidden slides 8 and 15 marked with gray placeholder. Slide 12 flagged for text overflow review.

best_practices: Always validate XML after editing PPTX files before repacking to prevent corruption
Use the html2pptx workflow for new presentations to ensure accurate positioning and styling
Create thumbnail previews after generation to catch layout issues before delivery
Back up original files before performing XML-level edits on existing presentations
anti_patterns: Never manually edit XML without running validation - always use validate.py after changes
Avoid using non-web-safe fonts in HTML slides as they may not render correctly in PowerPoint
Do not place charts or tables in single-column vertical layouts - use two-column or full-slide layouts instead
Never skip the visual validation step - always generate thumbnails to verify slide appearance

faq.question: What dependencies do I need to install?
faq.answer: You need Node.js with pptxgenjs and playwright packages, plus Python 3 with defusedxml and lxml. For thumbnail generation, install LibreOffice and poppler-utils.

faq.question: Can this skill edit existing PowerPoint templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can unpack template PPTX files, analyze their structure, duplicate slides, and modify content while preserving the original design and layouts.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: Primary support is for .pptx (PowerPoint). The underlying OOXML tools also support .docx (Word) and .xlsx (Excel) for document editing workflows.

faq.question: How do I handle charts and graphs?
faq.answer: For new presentations, define chart placeholder areas in HTML then use PptxGenJS to add chart data. For existing files, edit chart XML directly in the unpacked structure.

faq.question: Can this skill extract speaker notes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can read notesSlides/notesSlide{N}.xml files to extract speaker notes, and can also add or modify notes during the editing process.

faq.question: What if the generated slides have layout issues?
faq.answer: Use the thumbnail generation feature to visually identify problems. Common fixes include adjusting HTML margins, ensuring proper flexbox layouts, and verifying text doesn't overflow slide boundaries.
